2605.20088.title: INSHAPE: Instance-Level Shapelets for Interpretable Time-Series Classification
2605.20088.paper_summary: Discovering shapelets -- i.e., discriminative temporal patterns within time series -- has been widely studied to address the inherent complexity of time-series classification (TSC) and to make model decision-making processes more transparent. However, existing methods primarily focus on population-level shapelets optimized across the entire dataset, which leads to two fundamental limitations: (i) population-level patterns often misalign with instance-specific features, resulting in suboptimal performance and potentially misleading interpretations, and (ii) most methods treat shapelets as independent entities, overlooking important temporal dependencies and interactions among multiple patterns. To address these limitations, we propose INSHAPE, an interpretable TSC framework that discovers variable-length, discriminative temporal patterns specific to each time series. INSHAPE identifies these patterns as non-overlapping segments and models their temporal dependencies, thereby providing clear instance-level interpretations while achieving strong predictive performance. Furthermore, INSHAPE bridges local and global interpretability through a bottom-up approach, aggregating instance-level shapelets into prototypical (population-level) shapelets. Extensive experiments on 128 UCR and 30 UEA benchmark datasets show that INSHAPE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art shapelet-based methods while providing more intuitive and interpretable insights.
2605.20088.paper_summary_zh: 發現形狀元——即時間序列中的區別性時間模式——已被廣泛研究，以解決時間序列分類（TSC）固有的複雜性並使模型決策過程更加透明。<lb/>然而，現有方法主要集中在跨整個數據集優化的人口級形狀元，這導致了兩個基本限制：（i）人口級模式往往與實例特定特徵不對齊，導致次優性能和潛在的誤導性解釋，及（ii）大多數方法將形狀元視為獨立實體，忽略了多個模式之間重要的時間依賴性和互動。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們提出了INSHAPE，一個可解釋的TSC框架，發現特定於每個時間序列的變長區別性時間模式。<lb/>INSHAPE將這些模式識別為不重疊的段並建模它們的時間依賴性，從而提供清晰的實例級解釋，同時實現強大的預測性能。<lb/>此外，INSHAPE通過自下而上的方法橋接了局部和全局可解釋性，將實例級形狀元聚合為原型（人口級）形狀元。<lb/>在128個UCR和30個UEA基準數據集上的廣泛實驗顯示，INSHAPE始終超越最先進的基於形狀元的方法，同時提供更直觀和可解釋的見解。
2605.20088.author: Seongjun Lee et.al.
2605.20088.authors: Seongjun Lee, Seokhyun Lee, Changhee Lee
